00-index.html.title: 00-Index
00-index.html.description:
AI-powered news agency that detects newsworthy facts from Data360's 12,000 indicators and delivers them verified with local perspective to LAC newsrooms — operationalizing information integrity through authoritative sourcing, source traceability, and AI-readiness. A prototype by Abrimos.info for the Data360 Global Challenge (World Bank and Media Party), phase 2.
Code Repository URL: https://github.com/Abrimos-info/abrimos-data360-monitor/
Live demo: https://data360-demo.abrimos.info/
Installation Instructions: Installation Guide
Full documentation: https://abrimos.info/abrimos-data360-monitor/data360-news-agent/00-index.html
YouTube Link: https://youtu.be/HjJn9drlxdc Data360 News Agent makes an explicit distinction between reused battle-tested infrastructure (agentic LLM framework, newsletter distribution layer, and TeseoETL — our ingestion engine in production since 2019, which the production connector extends) and substantial new development: five novel detection strategies for development indicators (two live in this demo, three on the roadmap), a Fact-Checker Agent with veto power over every numeric claim, and a citizen-narrative module (Noticia + Reportaje) built from scratch for this prototype. The system operationalises Craig Hammer's criteria — data reliability, trustworthiness, and discoverability — through Proof-Carrying Numbers (PCN): every published figure is bound to a verifiable {indicator, country, date, value, endpoint} tuple and resolves back to the Data360 source in two clicks. Interpretability is a precondition for emission: the Fact-Checker Agent declares data gaps rather than imputing values, in explicit alignment with IFCN principles of transparency of methodology. The pipeline augments newsrooms — it does not replace editors: every alert surfaces as a reviewable draft, with partner editors retaining full editorial override before publication. Open-source models (Llama, Mistral, Qwen) are prioritised over closed APIs by design; the stack is self-hostable end to end; and the connector is country-agnostic — the five LAC countries in the demo are a focus choice, not an architectural constraint on a system ready to serve any of Data360's 200+ countries. Code: GPLv3.
→ Architecture OverviewThe Data360 integration is continuous and dynamic — never static snapshots: a conditional HEAD probe against each indicator's bulk CSV blob detects upstream changes on each pipeline run, and only changed indicators are selectively fetched, so every generated story is independently rebuildable from the API alone using indicator code, country code, date, value, and endpoint. This approach follows Craig Hammer's own framing of Data360 as a knowledge bank whose critical properties are data quality, authoritative sourcing, AI-readiness, and discoverability: Data360 is the primary and authoritative anchor of every published claim, and no intermediate cache substitutes for the source. Six endpoints are documented with their OData conventions and a reproducibility contract — any fact-checker, editor, or policymaker can replay the original API call and confirm or refute any figure. When an indicator does not exist for a given country-year, the system declares the gap explicitly; it does not interpolate or infer. The reference implementation is GPLv3, ensuring that this discoverability layer for the World Bank's knowledge bank remains a public good for newsrooms and institutions across the Global South.
→ Data360 API Integration MethodologyData360 News Agent is self-hosted by default — data sovereignty and cost predictability for public-interest newsrooms in the Global South are built-in design constraints, not afterthoughts — and grounded in battle-tested foundations: TeseoETL has been ingesting government data sources in production since 2019. The security protocol covers the full pipeline: public indicator data from Data360 (no personally identifiable information) is ingested dynamically with license references preserved in alert metadata; subscriber emails are stored in an append-only, gitignored file on the server filesystem, with a production roadmap for encrypted storage and one-click unsubscribe; chat input lives in browser sessionStorage only, never persisted server-side. LLM provider choice is configurable to avoid vendor lock-in: open-source models (Llama, Mistral, Qwen via LAIA/vLLM) are prioritised over closed APIs, and operators in any regulatory jurisdiction — EU, Brazil, Singapore — can run the entire stack against self-hosted open-weight models with no functional loss. Information integrity is operationalised, not invoked: the PCN verification pipeline drops any alert with an unverified claim before it reaches the published feed, and every numeric figure is independently verifiable in two clicks. Source code: GPLv3 — a deliberate choice ensuring the core verification layer stays freely accessible to public-interest media in the Global South.
→ Security & Data Handling ProtocolsThe user guide is written for the editor of a Latin American or Caribbean newsroom, not for a software team: it explains how to navigate the country front pages (newspaper layout with featured Reportaje and indicator headlines), how to verify PCN claim tokens against Data360 before publication, and how to use the global analytical chat to explore the full 12,000-indicator catalogue. The system produces two verified content types — Noticias (bilingual, 250–600 words, per indicator and country) and Reportajes (bilingual, 500–1,200 words, regional syntheses per dataset) — each carrying claim tokens that link every numeric figure to its authoritative Data360 source in two clicks, making data reliability and source traceability user-facing features, not backend properties. The editorial workflow is explicit: the system suggests, the newsroom decides — alerts surface as reviewable drafts; the editor verifies claim tokens, adds local context, and makes the final publication decision; AI augments the journalist, it does not replace the editor. Day-one distribution partners — Animal Político, Quinto Elemento Lab, and Ojoconmipisto — are integrated as readers of the alert feed, not as a logo wall. The guide is available in English and in Spanish.
→ User Guide (EN) · User Guide (ES)The sustainability plan presents Data360 News Agent as both a public good for public-interest media in the Global South and a B2B software product for data-publishing institutions — supported by three primary revenue lines: institutional implementation contracts (operated or enabled deployments for multilaterals, regional commissions, and national statistical offices); bilateral and foundation grants (the active IPI-funded Guatemalan procurement extension is the live precedent, proving the pipeline generalises beyond Data360 to other authoritative indicator catalogues); and premium editorial services for tier-1 LAC newsrooms. Unit economics are anchored in an observed reference run: steady-state daily cost on paid APIs runs approximately $5–6 for analysis, with a documented zero-cost fallback path (LAIA + NVIDIA NIM + Gemini Flash-Lite) that keeps the free tier viable for newsrooms in developing countries independent of any commercial contract. Editorial independence is a design property: GPLv3 ensures every derivative remains a public good; the PCN verification trace makes any published claim independently reproducible without going through Abrimos or its funders; and the receiving newsroom retains full editorial override over every generated story — the funder funds the pipeline, the newsroom owns the story. The roadmap milestones — SMTP newsletter, NiFi + OpenSearch scheduling, full activation of all five detection strategies, self-hosted LLM packaging — move the demo toward production without architectural refactor, and the connector's country-agnostic design means the same architecture is ready for any of the 200+ countries covered by Data360.
→ Sustainability & Maintenance Plan

01-installation-guide.html.title: 01-Installation-Guide
01-installation-guide.html.description:
AI-powered news agency that detects newsworthy facts from Data360's 12,000 indicators and delivers them verified with local perspective to LAC newsrooms — operationalizing information integrity through authoritative sourcing, source traceability, and AI-readiness. A prototype by Abrimos.info for the Data360 Global Challenge (World Bank and Media Party), phase 2.Ten-day sprint (21 to 31 May 2026). Submission deadline is 31 May 2026, via Media Party Hub.This is a substantial new development, not a minor modification of existing tooling. The foundation reuses battle-tested components from Sociedad.info (TeseoETL ingestion engine, agentic LLM framework, fact-checker logic, distribution layer). The following components are original to this prototype:The Data360 connector is country-agnostic: the demo covers five LAC countries by design choice, not by architectural constraint. The same connector reaches Data360's full 200+ country dataset. Frontend. Node.js, Pug templates, vanilla JavaScript — country front pages, articles, indicators hub, chat, newsletter preview Backend. Node.js HTTP server (data360-monitor.js)
Data layer. Data360 API v3 (REST + MCP optional in chat)
LLM. Multi-provider by design — open-source models (Llama, Mistral, Qwen via vLLM/LAIA and NVIDIA NIM) are the preferred self-hosted path for newsrooms; Claude Opus 4.7 (Anthropic) powers the demo pipeline via Agent SDK. Provider switching is a single env var (AI_PROVIDER). See lib/ai-client.js.
Verification. Proof-Carrying Numbers (PCN) from the World Bank Hosting. Abrimos-owned infrastructure
Production roadmap. Apache NiFi orchestrates continuous updates The monitor serves a newspaper-style product for five LAC countries:Subscribe modal → POST /api/subscribe → local TSV (data/newsletter/subscribers.tsv, gitignored). No real email in demo.Legacy card feed with client filters: /dev/feed or ?legacy=1 on a country page.
Full operator guide: docs/user-guide.md (ES) · docs/user-guide.en.md (EN).The demo runs two independent processes on the server: the Node monitor (dashboard + chat UI) and the Data360 MCP server (Python, chat tools only). Nginx terminates TLS and proxies only the monitor; MCP stays on localhost.
Nginx example. infra/nginx/data360.example.conf and infra/nginx/README.md.The chat agent calls Data360 through MCP (mcp_search_indicators, mcp_get_data, mcp_compare_countries, etc.). If MCP is down, lib/chat/tools.js falls back to the REST client automatically — the UI keeps working, but responses may be less enriched.1. Install MCP (once per host)
Requires Python 3.10+ and uv.git clone https://github.com/worldbank/data360-mcp.git cd data360-mcp uv sync uv pip install -e data360-mcp uv pip install -e data360-mcp-server Create .env in the MCP repo (or export):DATA360_API_BASE_URL=https://data360api.worldbank.org 2. Run MCPDevelopment:uv run fastmcp run data360-mcp-server/src/data360_mcp_server/main.py:mcp --transport http --port 8021 Or use the upstream script: ./run_server.sh.The HTTP endpoint must be reachable at http://127.0.0.1:8021/mcp (Streamable HTTP + SSE).3. Point the monitor at MCPIn this repo's .env:MCP_URL=http://127.0.0.1:8021/mcp Do not expose MCP through nginx — bind to localhost and let only the Node process call it.4. VerifyWith MCP running:node bin/evaluate-mcp.js Smoke-test from the deployed site: open an article chat and ask for a LAC comparison or a sparkline; tool traces should show source: data360_mcp.5. Production (systemd sketch)Run MCP as its own unit, restart independently of the monitor:# /etc/systemd/system/data360-mcp.service [Unit] Description=World Bank Data360 MCP server After=network.target [Service] Type=simple User=www-data WorkingDirectory=/opt/data360-mcp Environment=DATA360_API_BASE_URL=https://data360api.worldbank.org ExecStart=/usr/local/bin/uv run fastmcp run data360-mcp-server/src/data360_mcp_server/main.py:mcp --transport http --host 127.0.0.1 --port 8021 Restart=on-failure [Install] WantedBy=multi-user.target Adjust paths, then systemctl enable --now data360-mcp. Restart the monitor after changing MCP_URL.
For persistent hosting of the Node monitor, use PM2 with the included ecosystem file:npm install -g pm2 cp .env.example .env # configure D360_PORT, MCP_URL, AI_*, etc. pm2 start ecosystem.config.js pm2 save pm2 startup # optional: survive reboot
ecosystem.config.js runs data360-monitor.js with NODE_ENV=production, D360_PORT=8090, and loads secrets from .env via env_file. Run the Data360 MCP server separately (systemd or another PM2 app on port 8021).pm2 logs data360-monitor pm2 restart data360-monitor
Environment reference: docs/environment-variables.md.
With AI_PROVIDER=claude-code, the pipeline calls the Anthropic Messages API when ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is set. This is the expected path for local development and production (pm2).In .env:AI_PROVIDER=claude-code ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=sk-ant-api03-... CLAUDE_MODEL=opus
Create keys at console.anthropic.com. Full variable reference: docs/environment-variables.md.Guatemala, Honduras, Argentina, Ecuador, Mexico.Five canonical detection strategies cover development indicators. The demo implements two; the full architecture supports all five — each designed specifically for development indicators, not a generic anomaly detector.Continuous, dynamic ingestion via scheduled polling — not static snapshots. The pipeline uses bin/fetch-data.js to detect upstream updates before downloading; indicators are re-fetched only when the Data360 API reports a change (ETag-based).npm run fetch:probe # fast check (~2 s): which indicators changed? npm run fetch # probe + download changed + refresh LAC context Outputs. data/changed-since.json — indicators updated since the last probe (changed_indicators array) data/index.json — freshness summary for all watchlist indicators
Full design. docs/data-fetcher-architecture.mdDetection and narrative generation run in two phases inside one step:npm run fetch:news # optional: headlines for narrative context (Gemini default) npm run analyze # strategies 1 + 4 → Phase 1 Noticias → Phase 2 Reportajes → data/alerts.json The pipeline emits two content types: Noticia — bilingual news story (one per indicator/country, 250–600 words) triggered by a detection candidate. Reportaje — bilingual long-form (one per dataset, 500–1200 words) generated only when two or more Noticias share the same dataset_id. Synthesises a regional view and reuses the Noticias' claim_ids. Each alert carries a single country field (ISO3), not a list — see docs/alert-schema.json.Per-indicator only: node bin/generate-analysis.js --only FAO_CP_23012Indicators can be picked from the static 35-item watchlist (lib/watchlist.js) or discovered dynamically from /data360/searchv2:npm run pipeline # discover → fetch → news → analyze → newsletter npm run pipeline:force # same, but bypass the ETag cache npm run build # alias for pipeline npm run analyze:changed # analyze changed indicators only npm run analyze:noticias # Phase 1 only npm run analyze:reportajes # Phase 2 only npm run fetch:news # headlines (Gemini, dynamic watchlist) npm run pipeline:news-gdelt # GDELT fetch for watchlist npm run generate:newsletter # LAC newsletter edition for today (also run by pipeline) npm run replay:daily # day-by-day replay (fetch + analyze + newsletter)
Pipeline (AI_PROVIDER) and chat (CHAT_AI_PROVIDER): claude-code, vllm (LAIA), nvidia (NIM), OpenRouter. See .env.example and docs/environment-variables.md.
See docs/user-guide.md for the full operator reference. Working prototype and repo (this repository) Demo video (5 min)
Architecture overview. 03a-Architecture-Overview.md
Data360 API integration methodology. 03b-Data360-API-Integration-Methodology.md
Security and data handling. 03c-Security-Data-Handling-Protocols.md
User guide. 04b-User-Guide-ES.md (ES) · 04a-User-Guide-EN.md (EN) Features reference. docs/features-reference.md (repo)
Sustainability plan. 05-Sustainability-Maintenance-Plan.md Eduard Martín-Borregón. Lead, narrative, UX, video, documentation. Martin Szyszlican. Frontend, LLM agents, RAG, dashboard. Fernando Matzdorf. Data360 connector, schema, ETL. In the spirit of methodological transparency: Claude (Anthropic) and open-source LLMs (Llama, Mistral, Qwen) were used in building this prototype — for code, documentation drafts, and pipeline testing. All content published by the monitor can be reviewed and edited by a human journalist before distribution. The system is designed to augment newsroom workflows, not to replace editorial judgment.A declaration of AI use was included in our Expression of Interest (§20) and is reproduced here in keeping with our commitment to source traceability and transparency of methodology.
GPLv3 — a deliberate choice, not a default. The GPLv3 copyleft requirement ensures that every derivative of this system remains a public good: any fork, commercial adaptation, or institutional deployment must release its changes under the same terms.The core verification layer is, and will remain, freely accessible to public-interest media in developing countries.
See LICENSE.

03a-architecture-overview.html.title: 03a-Architecture-Overview
03a-architecture-overview.html.description:
Status. Final draft for Data360 Global Challenge phase 2 (2026-05-29). License. Code released under GPLv3; documentation under CC BY 4.0.
Data360 News Agent (repo abrimos-data360-monitor) is an AI-powered news agency that detects newsworthy facts across the 12,000 development indicators of World Bank Data360 and delivers them — verified and with local perspective — to Latin American and Caribbean newsrooms.
The system treats Data360 as an authoritative source and turns the World Bank's catalogue into a discoverable, AI-ready feed for public-interest media. It monitors indicators, detects statistically and editorially significant changes, generates bilingual narratives (Noticia + Reportaje), and publishes them with Proof-Carrying Numbers (PCN) so every numeric claim resolves back to the originating Data360 observation in two clicks.The framing follows Craig Hammer's lens on Data360: "As AI capabilities continue to grow, data reliability, trustworthiness, and discoverability rise to the level of critical priorities for the World Bank Group." — Craig Hammer, World Bank, Data360 Global Challenge launch, 2026-02-16. This architecture operationalises that principle: data reliability through the PCN trace; trustworthiness through interpretable, human-in-the-loop generation; discoverability through the agency feed that surfaces indicators the press has not yet covered.The challenge's terms of reference distinguish substantial new development from minor modifications. This prototype combines battle-tested infrastructure with a substantial new analytical and verification layer built specifically for development indicators.The substantial new development is architectural and analytical, not cosmetic: five novel detection strategies, a dedicated cross-domain verification layer for numeric claims, and a citizen-narrative module that did not exist in our previous work.The submission demo runs in historical replay mode: CSV snapshots are fetched once, analysis runs offline, and the web app reads a static data/alerts.json. There is no NiFi orchestrator, no OpenSearch index and no continuous polling loop in the demo — these belong to the production roadmap below. Being explicit about this boundary is part of the integrity contract with the jury.Two indicator-selection modes share the same pipeline: Static watchlist — ~35 indicators in lib/watchlist.js (pulse, annual, forecast tiers on disk). Dynamic discovery — datasets updated in the last N days via /data360/searchv2, expanded to indicator codes, stored as a fourth tier (dynamic). Default analysis uses NOTICIA_TIERS = ['dynamic']. ┌─────────────────┐ ┌──────────────────┐ ┌─────────────────┐ │ discover │ │ fetch-data.js │ │ fetch-news.js │ │ (optional) │────▶│ HEAD probe + CSV │────▶│ headlines JSONL │ └─────────────────┘ └────────┬─────────┘ └────────┬────────┘ │ │ ▼ ▼ ┌──────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ generate-analysis.js │ │ 1. detect (z-score, cross-indicator) │ │ 2. Phase 1: Noticia per indicator │ │ 3. Phase 2: Reportaje per dataset (≥2) │ │ 4. Fact-Checker Agent (PCN binding) │ └────────────────────┬─────────────────────┘ │ ▼ data/alerts.json │ ▼ ┌──────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ data360-monitor.js (Node + Pug) │ │ • Country front pages │ │ • Article pages + scoped chat │ │ • Global chat + MCP tools │ │ • Newsletter preview + subscribe TSV │ └──────────────────────────────────────────┘ Freshness probe — conditional HEAD on bulk CSV blobs; writes data/changed-since.json. This is the dynamic, not static contact with the API: each run reconciles only what moved at the source. Selective fetch — downloads changed indicators; refreshes data/context/{COUNTRY}/{tier}.csv and data/snapshots/. News ingest (optional) — GDELT and/or Gemini-backed fetch to data/news/{COUNTRY}/{YYYY-MM}.jsonl for LLM context (passive context for the narrative agent, not a detection input). Detection — strategies 1 (abrupt change) and 4 (cross-indicator anomaly) over LAC CSV context in the demo; strategy 3 (invisible trend) is partially surfaced through the newsletter selection scoring; the remaining two patterns are scaffolded in lib/detect/ and activated on the production roadmap. Narrative generation with interpretability — one LLM call per Noticia; one per Reportaje when ≥2 Noticias share a dataset_id. Output is validated against a deterministic schema (Q1 claim trace, Q2 schema, Q4 bilingual length) before it can be emitted. No claim is generated without a verifiable {indicator, country, date, value, endpoint} tuple. Emit — merge into data/alerts.json; enrich with display metadata and canonical article URLs. The novelty of this prototype is not in the UI; it is in the editorial questions the system asks of an authoritative time-series feed. The five strategies map one-to-one to the patterns a development-data editor cares about:Two of the five strategies are fully implemented (1 and 4); a third (invisible trend) is partially active through the newsletter selection logic, which surfaces multi-country patterns under-covered by the press via a composite score with an inverse-coverage signal; the remaining two are scaffolded and slated for the post-Challenge roadmap. This is the honest boundary — we report what the system can verify, not what it could rhetorically claim.Interpretability is not a marketing add-on; it is a precondition for emission.┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ Fact-Checker Agent (lib/analysis/quality-validator.js, │ │ lib/pcn-verify.js) │ │ │ │ Rule-based gates (deterministic): │ │ • Q1 claim_id resolves to a Data360 observation │ │ • Q2 output schema validates (bilingual fields, IDs) │ │ • Q4 word-count bounds per locale │ │ │ │ LLM reasoning (with source citation obligation): │ │ • numeric values match the cited indicator/date │ │ • directionality of change matches the underlying CSV │ │ • when the indicator is absent for country/year, the │ │ system declares the gap rather than imputing │ │ │ │ Default decision: REJECT. Emission requires PASS. │ └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ The agent has veto power before publication. Honesty about absent data is a feature: when an indicator does not exist for a country-year, the system declares the gap rather than inventing a value. This aligns with the IFCN principles of transparency of methodology and openness about limits.The system augments newsrooms; it does not replace editors. Every alert is published to the feed as a draft surface: partner editors read, edit and decide whether to use it in their own outlets. The UI is built around editing, not one-click publishing. The newsroom keeps the last call. ┌──────────────────────────────────┐ │ Apache NiFi (orchestrator) │ │ Schedules and triggers updates │ └────────────┬─────────────────────┘ │ invokes ▼ ┌──────────────────────────────────┐ │ TeseoETL fetcher script │ │ Calls Data360 API v3 │ │ Exports JSONlines per indicator │ └────────────┬─────────────────────┘ │ writes .jsonl ▼ ┌──────────────────────────────────┐ │ NiFi pipeline │ │ Reads JSONlines │ │ Normalises records │ │ Pushes to OpenSearch │ └────────────┬─────────────────────┘ │ indexes ▼ ┌──────────────────────────────────┐ │ OpenSearch │ │ Canonical observation store │ └────────────┬─────────────────────┘ │ queries ▼ ┌──────────────────────────────────┐ │ Detection and narrative layer │ │ Five strategies │ │ LLM call per indicator/dataset │ │ Fact-Checker Agent (PCN) │ └────────────┬─────────────────────┘ │ emits ▼ ┌──────────────────────────────────┐ │ Dashboard (Node.js/Pug) │ │ PCN verification marks │ │ Newsletter delivery (SMTP) │ └──────────────────────────────────┘ Not in demo (31 May 2026): NiFi scheduling, TeseoETL as the ingestion engine, OpenSearch indexing, continuous polling, automated email delivery, full activation of strategies 2, 3 and 5 (strategy 3 is partially surfaced via the newsletter).Standalone Node.js HTTP server. Default port 8090 (D360_PORT). Serves: Country picker and LAC front pages (newspaper layout) SEO-friendly article URLs per Noticia/Reportaje Indicators hub and per-IDNO detail pages Global analytical chat with tool traces (the discoverability surface for Data360) Scoped chat on article pages (generation context injected) Newsletter edition preview and subscription modal Static legal pages (/privacidad, /terminos, …) Detection: lib/detect/z-score.js, lib/detect/cross-indicator.js (strategies 1 and 4 live; 2, 3 and 5 scaffolded) Context: lib/analysis/context-builder.js — CSV tiers, indicator metadata, data dictionary, GDELT headlines, numbered claim candidates Phase 1: lib/analysis/runner.js — Noticia per indicator/country Phase 2: lib/analysis/reportaje-runner.js — Reportaje per dataset Quality: lib/analysis/quality-validator.js, lib/pcn-verify.js Output: data/alerts.json consumed by UI and chat tools The agentic framework benchmarks and orchestrates multiple models. Open-source models (Llama, Mistral, Qwen) are prioritised over closed ones (Claude, GPT-4) by design: closed models remain available as a fallback for benchmarking, but the production target is full self-hosting. This protects newsroom data sovereignty and keeps the system defensible under public scrutiny. REST (default for fetch/detection): lib/data360-client.js — search, metadata, CSV HEAD/GET, /data360/data
MCP (optional for chat): worldbank/data360-mcp on localhost; lib/mcp-client.js with REST fallback in lib/chat/tools.js The connector is country- and indicator-agnostic. The demo covers five LAC countries because focus is a design choice, not an architectural restriction — Data360 covers 200+ countries and the same code path serves any of them.Every numeric claim in narratives carries a claim_id resolvable to a Data360 observation. The pipeline drops alerts whose claims fail verification. The frontend renders {{claim:…}} tokens via @pcn-js/core patterns and local helpers, so a reader can travel from any number in the published text back to the originating indicator card and the Data360 source endpoint in two clicks. Source traceability is operational, not aspirational. Fixture editions in data/newsletter/editions/ Subscription form → data/newsletter/subscribers.tsv (gitignored) No mail transport in demo; production adds SMTP + List-Unsubscribe + unsubscribe links Day-one distribution partners — Animal Político, Quinto Elemento Lab, Ojoconmipisto — are integrated as readers of the alert feed, not as a logo wall Two independent processes on the server:
Nginx terminates TLS and proxies only the monitor. MCP binds to 127.0.0.1; not exposed publicly. Staging may use HTTP basic auth — see infra/nginx/README.md.The stack is self-hostable end to end, including the LLM layer. This is the unit-economic and sovereignty argument: a newsroom in the Global South can run the system on its own infrastructure without recurrent licensing costs and without sending editorial data to third-party APIs.Environment: .env for AI_PROVIDER, CHAT_AI_PROVIDER, MCP_URL, NVIDIA_API_KEY, etc. (see .env.example).Stating the boundary is the integrity contract. The architecture is designed so each line of this table moves from left to right without refactor.This architecture is the seed of a regional knowledge bank layer for Data360: a feed that turns the World Bank's catalogue into editable material for public-interest media across the Global South. The licence (GPLv3) guarantees that every derivative remains a public good. The connector is endpoint-agnostic by design, the verification layer is portable across numeric-claim domains, and the partner network is real. What ships on 31 May is the working core; what the architecture enables is a replicable model for AI-ready, authoritatively sourced civic journalism wherever Data360 reaches.

03b-data360-api-integration-methodology..title: 03b-Data360-API-Integration-Methodology
03b-data360-api-integration-methodology..description:
Status. Final draft (2026-05-29). Released under CC BY 4.0. Reference implementation released under GPLv3. "As AI capabilities continue to grow, data reliability, trustworthiness, and discoverability rise to the level of critical priorities for the World Bank Group." — Craig Hammer, World Bank, Data 360 Global Challenge launch, 2026-02-16. This document specifies how the prototype treats the Data360 API as an authoritative source for an AI-ready newsroom pipeline. The integration is continuous and dynamic — scheduled polling against the live API, never static snapshots — so that every generated story is rebuildable from the API alone: indicator code, country code, date, value, endpoint. No intermediate cache, dump, or mirror substitutes for the source; the cached blobs described below are a freshness optimization, not a substitute. Data360 is the primary anchor of every published claim; supplementary sources (e.g. OCDS) only enter through a separate verification layer and never carry the analytical weight.Operational reading: this is the deliverable that demonstrates Criterion 2 (Effective Use of World Bank Data360) and supports Criterion 5 (Feasibility & Technical Robustness). It aligns with Hammer's own framing — data quality, authoritative sourcing, AI-readiness, discoverability — and treats Data360 as the knowledge bank whose discoverability into LAC newsrooms is the system's reason to exist.
The bulk CSV URL is {BLOB_BASE}/{databaseId}/{idno}.csv, with idno used exactly as returned by listIndicators. The static watchlist happens to use idnos that start with the database id (e.g. WB_WDI_* under WB_WDI), but this is a property of the values the API returns, not a separate convention. Dynamic discovery follows the same rule. See csvUrl in lib/data360-client.js.Data360 does not expose a bulk "updated since" feed. To keep ingestion dynamic — the system must react to upstream change, not consume a frozen export — the monitor detects upstream changes with conditional HEAD on each indicator's bulk CSV blob, on a scheduled cadence. Probe. HEAD the CSV URL with cached If-None-Match / If-Modified-Since. Interpret. 304 = unchanged. 200 = changed (or first probe). Report. Write data/changed-since.json with the list of changed idno values. Fetch selectively. Download CSV snapshots and refresh LAC context only for changed indicators. The metadata field series_description.date_last_update is null for all probed watchlist indicators (2026-05-21). Do not use it for change detection. The reliable signal is the blob Last-Modified header.
See docs/data-fetcher-architecture.md for schemas, commands, and module map. Abrupt changes. Z-score against historical trajectory per (indicator, country, disaggregation). Cross-indicator anomalies. Outliers from the regional pattern within the LAC scope. MCP runs on localhost only in production layout. Evaluate with node bin/evaluate-mcp.js.Public UI links use https://data360.worldbank.org/en/indicator/{IDNO} via lib/data360-urls.js and static/js/data360-urls.js. Dataset pages use /en/search?query={DATABASE_ID}.POST /data360/metadata with filter on indicator idno — used when enriching indicator pages and context builder. See lib/data360-client.js → getMetadata().POST /data360/searchv2 with orderby: 'series_description/date_last_update desc', then GET /data360/indicators?datasetId=X for canonical idno values. Implemented in lib/dynamic-watchlist.js.(Further expansion: full OData field mapping, disaggregation rules, and CSV column contract — see docs/data-fetcher-architecture.md.)Every alert and every citizen narrative produced by the system is independently rebuildable from the Data360 API alone. The verification trace attached to each claim records: idno — the canonical indicator identifier as returned by listIndicators country — ISO-3 code date — observation timestamp value — the observed figure endpoint — the API path and parameters used to retrieve it retrieved_at — UTC timestamp of the call Any third party — a fact-checker at Poynter, an editor at a partner newsroom, a policymaker reviewing a brief — can replay the call and confirm or refute the figure. When the indicator does not exist for a given country-year, the system declares the gap explicitly; it does not interpolate, infer, or substitute. This is what AI-ready means operationally for this prototype: every model output is anchored to a retrievable record in the authoritative source.The integration is designed to widen Data360's reach into LAC newsroom workflows without re-mediating its authority. The connector is country-agnostic and dataset-agnostic; the MVP focuses on five LAC countries and a curated indicator family because we chose focus, not because the architecture restricts it. Each published story functions as a discoverability event for the underlying indicator — readers click from the story into the Data360 record in two steps — turning the knowledge bank into a feed that public-interest media can edit, contextualize, and distribute under their own editorial control. The reference implementation is GPLv3 so that every derivative remains a public good.

03c-security-data-handling-protocols.htm.title: 03c-Security-Data-Handling-Protocols
03c-security-data-handling-protocols.htm.description:
Status. Final draft for Data360 Global Challenge phase 2 (2026-05-29). Data360 News Agent is an AI-based news agency that detects newsworthy facts across the 12,000 indicators of Data360 and delivers them — verified and contextualised for LAC newsrooms. This document describes how the system handles data, user input, and third-party services across the pipeline that produces those alerts and citizen narratives. It is written for the Data360 Global Challenge jury.The architecture is self-hosted by default: data sovereignty and cost predictability for public-interest newsrooms in the Global South are design constraints, not afterthoughts. The pipeline is built on battle-tested foundations — TeseoETL has been ingesting government data sources in production since 2019 — and shields every step from indicator ingestion to claim publication, so that Data360's authoritative sourcing remains intact as it flows toward the reader.All economic and social indicators come from World Bank Data360, treated as the authoritative primary source for every published claim. Ingestion is dynamic — not static snapshots: the connector polls the API on a schedule, so each generated story is rebuildable from the indicator code, country code, date and value alone. The demo does not ingest private datasets, credentials, or personally identifiable information from indicator sources. New component (red block in the architecture overview): the Data360 connector — an extension of TeseoETL with parsers, normalisation and change detection tailored to development indicators. The rest of the ingestion stack is reused (green) and has been hardened over years of production use. License compliance: each indicator carries a license field (typically CC BY 4.0). The pipeline preserves license references in alert metadata and verification traces, so that downstream readers can trace every value back to Data360 with data reliability, trustworthiness and discoverability intact.Headlines are fetched from GDELT Document API v2 and/or Gemini-assisted search (see bin/fetch-news.js). Stored fields include title, URL, date, and source domain — all public web content. No paywalled full-text storage in the demo.When a visitor submits the subscription modal:Storage: append-only TSV at data/newsletter/subscribers.tsv. This file is gitignored and lives only on the server filesystem.Demo scope (intentional minimum): a single real send is captured on the day of submission; no automated mailing, no double opt-in confirmation, no automated unsubscribe flow yet. The UI states this clearly; full policy text lives at /privacidad (loaded from config/copy/privacidad.{es,en}.json).Production roadmap: self-hostable mail relay (no vendor lock-in), encrypted storage at rest, GDPR-style deletion on request, one-click unsubscribe in every message, and per-newsroom data residency for partners with local regulatory constraints.Chat augments the journalist — it surfaces verified context from Data360, but every published claim still flows through the newsroom editor before it leaves the building. Questions typed in global or article-scoped chat are sent to the configured LLM provider for the current session. In demo: Chat input is not linked to subscription email (no account system). Conversation history for article chat is stored in browser sessionStorage only — not on the server after the SSE response completes. Global chat history is held in page memory for the session; not persisted server-side. No chat answer is auto-published; the chat is an editorial assistant, not an autopilot. The pipeline is interpretable by design: every claim a model emits is anchored to a retrieved Data360 record, and no claim leaves the pipeline without a verifiable indicator, value, timestamp and API endpoint. Provider choice is configurable to avoid vendor lock-in, with a deliberate preference for self-hostable open-source models (Llama, Mistral, Qwen via LAIA / vLLM) over closed APIs (Claude, GPT-4) — the closed providers exist as benchmarking and fallback options, not as architectural dependencies.Configured via environment variables — see the full secrets and configuration table in docs/environment-variables.md (repo) (AI_PROVIDER, CHAT_AI_PROVIDER, GEMINI_API_KEY, NVIDIA_API_KEY, etc.):Tool results sent to any LLM contain public indicator values and alert summaries — never subscriber emails. Operators reviewing the system for a specific jurisdiction (EU, Brazil, Singapore) can run the entire stack against self-hosted open-source models with no functional loss, which is the regulatory escape hatch built into the architecture.Optional Python MCP server (worldbank/data360-mcp) runs on localhost only (127.0.0.1:8021). The Node monitor calls it for chat tools; it is not exposed through nginx or the public internet.When npm run fetch:news uses Gemini, article titles/URLs may be sent to Google's API for grounding or summarization. API keys belong in .env (gitignored).The demo does not use: Third-party analytics (Google Analytics, Meta Pixel, etc.) Advertising trackers Cross-site tracking cookies Language preference and onboarding dismissal use first-party browser storage (localStorage / query params) only. No cookie banner is required for the demo scope; the privacy page at /privacidad documents this.Every numeric value in generated narratives maps to a verifiable claim token with a claim_id. Information integrity is operationalised, not invoked: the system refuses to publish any claim without a verifiable Data360 anchor.lib/pcn-verify.js checks each claim against data/context/{COUNTRY}/{tier}.csv for tiers in CONTEXT_TIERS (annual, forecast, dynamic). A 0.1% numeric tolerance handles rounding. MCP-sourced 8-char IDs skip ID consistency check and compare values directly. Alerts with any unverified claim are dropped before writing data/alerts.json.@pcn-js/core and @pcn-js/data360 (Apache-2.0) support claim mark rendering. Every published claim is independently verifiable in two clicks: indicator card → Data360 source URL in verification_trace. This is the user-facing surface of the Shielding the Pipeline principle: the reader, not just the editor, can audit any number on the page. Infrastructure: Abrimos-owned servers in the demo; the deployment recipe is fully self-hostable by any partner newsroom, with no proprietary dependencies along the critical path. Data sovereignty for newsrooms in developing countries is the default, not an upgrade tier. TLS: HTTPS in production/staging via nginx. Staging gate: optional HTTP basic auth on the monitor only (infra/nginx/.htpasswd — demo credentials documented as intentionally weak). Secrets: API keys and provider tokens in .env, never committed (see .gitignore). Full variable list: docs/environment-variables.md (repo). Static assets: served from /static/* with cache headers; no user data in static files. MCP and subscriber TSV are not reachable from the public internet in the recommended deployment layout.
Source: abrimos-info/abrimos-data360-monitor under GPLv3 — a deliberate choice, not a default. Strong copyleft ensures every derivative remains a public good and the core verification layer stays freely accessible to public-interest media in the Global South. Generated alerts and analysis artifacts in data/ may be committed for demo replay; subscriber TSV is excluded.
Contact for data requests: contacto@abrimos.info (referenced on /privacidad). *Chat may contain editorial questions that indirectly identify a newsroom; demo does not authenticate users or log chat server-side.The protocols above are not a compliance exercise. They are the substrate that makes the rest of the proposal credible: a self-hostable, open-source, AI-ready agent that turns Data360 — the World Bank's authoritative knowledge bank — into a discoverable feed for LAC newsrooms, without forcing those newsrooms to surrender data sovereignty or editorial independence in exchange for the tool.That substrate is what allows partner newsrooms to adopt the agent without renegotiating their own governance, and what allows the same architecture to replicate to other regions and other indicator families with no change to the security model. Reliability, trustworthiness and discoverability — the criteria the World Bank has named as critical priorities — are protected end-to-end: from API call to claim mark on the published page.

04a-user-guide-en.html.title: 04a-User-Guide-EN
04a-user-guide-en.html.description:
Language · English · Versión en español Audience: newsrooms in Latin America and the Caribbean. Status: functional draft as of 2026-05-29 (phase 2 demo, submission 2026-05-31). Data360 News Agent is an AI-powered news agency that detects newsworthy facts in Data360's 12,000 indicators and delivers them verified with local perspective to LAC newsrooms.The system monitors economic, social, and governance indicators in Guatemala, Honduras, Argentina, Ecuador, and Mexico. When it detects a statistically notable fact, it publishes two types of verified content: Noticia — bilingual piece (250–600 words) per indicator and country, triggered by a detection candidate. Reportaje — bilingual long-form piece (500–1200 words) per dataset, generated only when two or more Noticias share the same dataset_id. Synthesises a regional view with per-country sections and reuses the claim_ids from the Noticias it synthesises.
Every piece links back to its source at data360.worldbank.org.The product has three main surfaces:All views read the same alerts file (data/alerts.json). Information integrity principle. Every published claim is independently verifiable in two clicks: indicator card → source in Data360. The system generates no claim without a verifiable indicator, value, timestamp, and API endpoint — data reliability, trustworthiness, and discoverability are the core of the product, not a secondary feature. Newsrooms integrate this alert feed as an editorial starting point, applying their own publication criteria. Countries: GTM, HND, ARG, ECU, MEX (do not substitute BRA, CHL, COL, etc.). Indicators: two selection modes. Static watchlist: ~35 indicators across three update categories (pulse, annual, forecasts). Dynamic mode (recommended): indicators discovered live from the Data360 API and updated continuously. This is the active default source. Detection: strategies 1 (abrupt changes, z-score) and 4 (cross-indicator anomalies). Mode: historical replay over CSV snapshots, not continuous real time (D-007). Headlines: GDELT and Gemini integration for pre-existing media coverage context. The narrative–data divergence strategy is designed; active on the roadmap. Top bar (World Bank theme): Front page — country picker and LAC front pages Global indicators — /indicadores hub About — methodology, limits, team, license Subscribe — subscription modal (LAC newsletter or indicator alerts) ES / EN — UI and narrative language selector Legal and informational pages: /metodologia, /privacidad, /terminos, /uso (content in config/copy/, not covered here).ES / EN selector in the bar. Changes the interface and the language of displayed narratives. Pieces store text in both languages in JSON; the UI shows one language at a time (no simultaneous bilingual mode on screen).URL parameters: ?lang=es or ?lang=en.Welcome modal on first visit (dismissible). Presents Data360 News Agent as an AI news agency: it detects newsworthy facts in Data360's 12,000 indicators and delivers them verified with local perspective to LAC newsrooms. Force with ?onboarding=1.The Subscribe button opens a modal with two types:In the demo, the form saves email + type (+ optional countries/topics for alerts) to data/newsletter/subscribers.tsv via POST /api/subscribe. No real email is sent and there is no SMTP confirmation (roadmap).Valid alert topics: macro, fiscal, social, food_security, governance. Links to the five LAC front pages (/guatemala, /honduras, …). List of recently updated indicators with links to the hub or article. Legacy deep link: /?alert={id} redirects to the canonical article if _path exists. Newspaper layout (frontpage.pug template): Featured reportaje — in-depth piece in the hero (if a reportaje exists for that country). Headlines — noticias interleaved with reportajes (interleaveHeadlines()); link to the article. Updated indicators — horizontal bar with chips (value + magnitude + sparkline); link to the noticia. Masthead — edition label (month · year) and data age. Ordered by data date (time_period), not detection date.Each headline or chip shows indicator code (IDNO), data date (stale badge if applicable), detection type, and narrative in the active language.Country picker popup (country-menu.js) to jump between LAC front pages.Available at /dev/feed or by adding ?legacy=1 to a country front page. Renders the classic SSR feed (dashboard.pug) with client-side filters:Filters hide cards with CSS; the event counter updates instantly. State in URL (?country=ARG&category=economy&variant=narr).Reading page with: Headline, lead, full story with resolved PCN tokens Historical sparkline, value, period, magnitude Traceability: Data360 link, CSV, methodology Editorial use disclaimer and production metadata Newsletter subscription CTA in the footer Each article includes a scoped chat FAB (floating-chat.pug + alert-chat.js). The conversation is stored in sessionStorage by alert_id; on reload, markdown, tool traces, cached sparklines, and activity steps are restored.The agent uses lib/prompts/chat-scoped-system.md and receives: Published piece — headline, lead, story, and claim_ids from JSON. Generation context — omnibus markdown from data/analyses/{IDNO}.md (fallback *.llm-call.md). For reportajes, the .md files of each indicator in the dataset are concatenated. Limit: CHAT_GENERATION_CONTEXT_MAX_CHARS (default 48000).Hub with indicators grouped by tier (annual, forecast, dynamic), alert count per IDNO, recent indicators, and URL filters (?countries=ARG,MEX&topics=macro).Per-indicator detail: /indicador/{IDNO} — metadata, alerts by country, links to articles. /newsletter redirects to the most recent LAC edition in data/newsletter/editions/. Each fixture edition includes hero, secondary headlines, bilingual subject/preheader, and CTA to front page or article. Linked from the subscription modal and article footers. Choose a preset or type a question. Optional: adjust Focus country (dropdown). The agent calls pipeline tools (live SSE). The response includes markdown, sparklines, alert cards, and source traceability. All or one country (GTM · Guatemala, etc.) in the dropdown. Each query sends focus_countries to the server; if you changed focus, the agent receives an explicit note.Recently published data (N) opens a panel with indicators whose CSV changed (data/changed-since.json). Per row: tier, IDNO, blob date, Chart and Analysis buttons.Copy markdown, download .md, print PDF (chat-export.js). Does not invent figures; must call Data360 tools before stating numbers. Headlines: must call fetch_news / read_news. Analysis: list_alerts first; run_analysis only if no alerts on disk. Charts: mcp_get_data + sparkline block. Monitor links: deep link to canonical article or /?alert=ID. Static page covering: what the product is, LAC scope, detection methodology, PCN verification, demo limitations, roadmap (NiFi, OpenSearch, Strategy 2), Abrimos.info team, and GPLv3 license.Formal schema: docs/alert-schema.json.Canonical URLs: /{countrySlug}/{noticia|reportaje}/{year}/{month}/{slug} (lib/url-slug.js).The system suggests; the newsroom decides. The agent produces a verified draft; the editor assesses newsworthiness, adds local context, and makes the publication decision. Receive the alert — the feed shows indicator, value, magnitude of change, and data date. Read the narrative — review the draft; check the angle against existing coverage (the global chat can show GDELT headlines by country). Verify the claim tokens — each numbered claim links to its source in Data360. Follow Sources: dataset → CSV → methodology. Confirm the period — time_period is the data date, not the detection date. Publish based on the actual data date. Edit and publish — adjust tone, add local context, incorporate your own sources. Use Copy citation to paste text + URL into your CMS. The verification trace — the source chain linking every claim to its original Data360 record — is public and reproducible. Any reader can verify the full chain: claim → indicator → API → methodology. More methodological context: docs/data360-integration-methodology.md.npm run fetch # download CSV + data dictionary + meta.json npm run fetch:probe # change probe only (~2 s) npm run fetch:news # headlines (Gemini, dynamic watchlist) npm run analyze # detection + Noticias + Reportajes → data/alerts.json npm run analyze:changed # analyze changed-since indicators only npm run analyze:noticias # Phase 1 only npm run analyze:reportajes # Phase 2 only npm run analyze:no-llm # pipeline without LLM npm run discover # /searchv2 → dynamic-watchlist.json npm run pipeline # discover → fetch → news → analyze → newsletter npm run pipeline:force # same, bypass ETag on fetch npm run build # alias for pipeline npm run pipeline:news-gdelt # GDELT fetch against watchlist npm run generate:newsletter # LAC edition for the day (also run at end of pipeline) npm run replay:daily # day-by-day historical replay (fetch + analyze + newsletter) npm run dev # web server :8090 (development) npm run start # production npm test # Node tests Full flow: npm run pipeline (or npm run build). Individual steps: fetch → fetch:news (optional) → analyze. Multi-day replay: replay:daily --from=2026-05-22 --to=2026-05-29 (respects CLAUDE_EFFORT).Main output: data/alerts.json.Full reference: docs/environment-variables.md (repo). Template: .env.example (repo).CLAUDE_EFFORT controls the Claude CLI --effort= flag for analysis, newsletter, and replay:daily. Default empty (no flag); replay:daily uses medium if unset.Each Noticia and Reportaje carries a single country field (ISO3), not a list. Formal schema: docs/alert-schema.json.templates/layout.pug loads static/css/layout.css in <head> (blocking) with tokens, WB theme, and chrome. Remaining page styles live in static/css/main.css and are included per template. This reduces FOUC on the app shell.Definitions: lib/chat/tools.js. No NiFi orchestration or OpenSearch at runtime. Chat depends on configured LLM; may hallucinate if it skips tools. Uneven GDELT coverage (HND/GTM weaker). Subscription saves email to local TSV; no email send or automated unsubscribe. Strategy 2 (headline–data divergence): designed, not active in detection. run_analysis can take minutes and has LLM cost. Roadmap: docs/architecture-overview.md, docs/sustainability-plan.md.The demo covers five Latin American and Caribbean countries as proof of concept. The Data360 connector is country-agnostic: the 200+ countries covered by Data360 are the real technical horizon, not a future promise — the prototype limits coverage by choice of focus, not by architectural constraint.The code is free under GPLv3: any derivative must remain a public good. The core verification layer is, and will remain, freely accessible to public-interest media in the Global South. The self-hosted design guarantees data sovereignty and predictable cost for newsrooms in developing countries.Full roadmap: docs/architecture-overview.md, docs/sustainability-plan.md.

04b-user-guide-es.html.title: 04b-User-Guide-ES
04b-user-guide-es.html.description:
Idioma · Español · English version Audiencia: redacciones de América Latina y el Caribe. Estado: borrador funcional al 2026-05-29 (demo fase 2, entrega 2026-05-31). Data360 News Agent es una agencia de noticias basada en inteligencia artificial que detecta hechos noticiosos en los 12.000 indicadores de Data360 y los entrega verificados con perspectiva local a newsrooms LAC.El sistema vigila indicadores económicos, sociales y de gobernanza en Guatemala, Honduras, Argentina, Ecuador y México. Cuando detecta un hecho estadísticamente notable, publica dos tipos de contenido verificado: Noticia. Pieza bilingüe (250–600 palabras) por indicador y país, disparada por un candidato de detección. Reportaje. Pieza bilingüe larga (500–1200 palabras) por dataset, generada solo cuando dos o más Noticias comparten el mismo dataset_id. Sintetiza una mirada regional con secciones por país y reutiliza los claim_id de las Noticias que sintetiza.
Cada pieza queda enlazada a su fuente en data360.worldbank.org.El producto tiene tres caras principales:Todas leen el mismo archivo de alertas (data/alerts.json). Principio de integridad informativa. Cada afirmación publicada es verificable en dos clics: tarjeta del indicador → fuente en Data360. El sistema no genera ningún claim sin un indicador, valor, fecha y endpoint de API verificables. La confiabilidad, rastreabilidad y descubribilidad de los datos son el núcleo del producto, no una característica accesoria. Medios de América Latina integran este feed como punto de partida editorial, con criterio propio de publicación. Países: GTM, HND, ARG, ECU, MEX (no sustituir por BRA, CHL, COL, etc.). Indicadores: dos modos de selección. Watchlist estático: ~35 indicadores en tres categorías de actualización (pulso, anual, proyecciones). Modo dinámico (recomendado): indicadores descubiertos en vivo desde el API Data360 y actualizados de forma continua. Es la fuente activa por defecto. Detección: estrategias 1 (cambios abruptos, z-score) y 4 (anomalías cross-indicador). Modo: replay histórico sobre snapshots CSV, no tiempo real continuo (D-007). Titulares: integración con GDELT y Gemini para contexto de cobertura mediática preexistente. La estrategia de divergencia narrativa-dato está diseñada; activa en roadmap. Barra superior (tema World Bank): Portada — selector de país y portadas LAC Indicadores globales — hub /indicadores About — metodología, límites, equipo, licencia Suscribirse — modal de suscripción (newsletter LAC o alertas por indicador) ES / EN — selector de idioma de interfaz y narrativas Páginas legales e informativas: /metodologia, /privacidad, /terminos, /uso (contenido en config/copy/, no en esta guía).Selector ES / EN en la barra. Cambia la interfaz y el idioma de las narrativas mostradas. Las piezas guardan texto en ambos idiomas en el JSON; la UI muestra un idioma a la vez (no hay modo bilingüe simultáneo en pantalla).Parámetros URL: ?lang=es o ?lang=en.Modal de bienvenida en la primera visita (dismissible). Presenta Data360 News Agent como agencia de noticias con IA: detecta hechos noticiosos en los 12.000 indicadores de Data360 y los entrega verificados con perspectiva local a newsrooms LAC. Forzar con ?onboarding=1.Botón Suscribirse abre un modal con dos tipos:En el demo, el formulario guarda email + tipo (+ países/temas opcionales para alertas) en data/newsletter/subscribers.tsv vía POST /api/subscribe. No se envía correo real ni confirmación SMTP (roadmap).Temas válidos para alertas: macro, fiscal, social, food_security, governance. Enlaces a las cinco portadas LAC (/guatemala, /honduras, …). Lista de indicadores recientemente actualizados con enlace al hub o al artículo. Deep link legacy: /?alert={id} redirige al artículo canónico si existe _path. Layout de periódico (template frontpage.pug): Reportaje destacado — pieza de profundidad en hero (si hay reportaje para ese país). Titulares — noticias intercaladas con reportajes (interleaveHeadlines()); enlace al artículo. Indicadores actualizados — barra horizontal con chips (valor + magnitud + sparkline); enlace a la noticia. Masthead — etiqueta de edición (mes · año) y antigüedad de datos. Orden por fecha del dato (time_period), no por fecha de detección.Cada titular o chip muestra código de indicador (IDNO), fecha del dato (badge stale si aplica), tipo de detección y narrativa según idioma activo.Selector de país en popup (country-menu.js) para saltar entre portadas LAC.Disponible en /dev/feed o añadiendo ?legacy=1 a una portada de país. Renderiza el feed SSR clásico (dashboard.pug) con filtros cliente:Los filtros ocultan tarjetas con CSS; el contador de eventos se actualiza al instante. Estado en URL (?country=ARG&category=economy&variant=narr).Página de lectura con: Titular, lead, story completa con tokens PCN resueltos Sparkline histórico, valor, periodo, magnitud Trazabilidad: enlace Data360, CSV, metodología Disclaimer de uso editorial y metadatos de producción CTA de suscripción al newsletter en el pie Cada artículo incluye un FAB de chat acotado (floating-chat.pug + alert-chat.js). La conversación se guarda en sessionStorage por alert_id y, al recargar, se restauran markdown, trace de tools, sparklines en caché y pasos de actividad.El agente usa lib/prompts/chat-scoped-system.md y recibe: Pieza publicada — titular, lead, story y claim_ids del JSON. Contexto de generación — markdown omnibus de data/analyses/{IDNO}.md (fallback *.llm-call.md). En reportajes se concatenan los .md de cada indicador del dataset. Límite: CHAT_GENERATION_CONTEXT_MAX_CHARS (default 48000).Hub con indicadores agrupados por tier (annual, forecast, dynamic), conteo de alertas por IDNO, indicadores recientes y filtros URL (?countries=ARG,MEX&topics=macro).Detalle por indicador: /indicador/{IDNO} — metadata, alertas por país, enlaces a artículos. /newsletter redirige a la edición LAC más reciente en data/newsletter/editions/. Cada edición fixture incluye hero, titulares secundarios, subject/preheader bilingüe y CTA a portada o artículo. Enlace desde el modal de suscripción y desde el pie de artículos. Elegí un preset o escribí una pregunta. Opcional: ajustá País foco (dropdown). El agente llama tools del pipeline (SSE en vivo). La respuesta incluye markdown, sparklines, tarjetas de alerta y trazabilidad de fuentes. Dropdown Todos o un país (GTM · Guatemala, etc.). En cada consulta se envía focus_countries al servidor; si cambiaste el foco, el agente recibe una nota explícita.Botón Datos recién publicados (N) abre panel con indicadores cuyo CSV cambió (data/changed-since.json). Por fila: tier, IDNO, fecha blob, botones Gráfica y Análisis.Copiar markdown, descargar .md, imprimir PDF (chat-export.js). No inventa cifras; debe llamar tools Data360 antes de afirmar números. Titulares: debe llamar fetch_news / read_news. Análisis: primero list_alerts; solo run_analysis si no hay alertas en disco. Gráficas: mcp_get_data + bloque sparkline. Enlaces al monitor: deep link al artículo canónico o /?alert=ID. Página estática con: qué es el producto, alcance LAC, metodología de detección, verificación PCN, limitaciones del demo, roadmap (NiFi, OpenSearch, Strategy 2), equipo Abrimos.info y licencia GPLv3.Esquema formal: docs/alert-schema.json.URLs canónicas: /{countrySlug}/{noticia|reportaje}/{year}/{month}/{slug} (lib/url-slug.js).El sistema sugiere; la redacción decide. El agente produce un borrador verificado; el editor evalúa noticiabilidad, ajusta contexto local y toma la decisión de publicar. Recibir la alerta — el feed muestra indicador, valor, magnitud del cambio y fecha del dato. Leer la narrativa — revisar el borrador; contrastar el ángulo con cobertura existente (el chat global puede mostrar titulares GDELT por país). Verificar los claim tokens — cada afirmación numerada enlaza a su fuente en Data360. Seguir Sources: dataset → CSV → metodología. Confirmar el periodo — el time_period es la fecha del dato, no la de detección. Publicar según la fecha real del dato. Editar y publicar — ajustar tono, agregar contexto local, incorporar fuentes propias. Usar Copiar cita para pegar texto + URL en el CMS. La verification trace — el rastro de fuentes que enlaza cada claim con su dato original en Data360 — es pública y reproducible. Cualquier lector puede verificar la cadena completa: afirmación → indicador → API → metodología. Más contexto metodológico: docs/data360-integration-methodology.md.npm run fetch # descarga CSV + data dictionary + meta.json npm run fetch:probe # solo sonda de cambios (~2 s) npm run fetch:news # titulares (Gemini, watchlist dinámico) npm run analyze # detección + Noticias + Reportajes → data/alerts.json npm run analyze:changed # analyze solo indicadores changed-since npm run analyze:noticias # solo Fase 1 npm run analyze:reportajes # solo Fase 2 npm run analyze:no-llm # pipeline sin LLM npm run discover # /searchv2 → dynamic-watchlist.json npm run pipeline # discover → fetch → news → analyze → newsletter npm run pipeline:force # igual, bypass ETag en fetch npm run build # alias de pipeline npm run pipeline:news-gdelt # fetch GDELT contra watchlist npm run generate:newsletter # edición LAC del día (también al final de pipeline) npm run replay:daily # replay histórico día a día (fetch + analyze + newsletter) npm run dev # servidor web :8090 (desarrollo) npm run start # producción npm test # tests Node Flujo completo: npm run pipeline (o npm run build). Pasos sueltos: fetch → fetch:news (opcional) → analyze. Replay multi-día: replay:daily --from=2026-05-22 --to=2026-05-29 (respeta CLAUDE_EFFORT).Salida principal: data/alerts.json.Referencia completa: docs/environment-variables.md (repo). Plantilla: .env.example (repo).CLAUDE_EFFORT controla el flag --effort= del CLI Claude en análisis, newsletter y replay:daily. Default vacío (sin flag); replay:daily usa medium si no se define.Cada Noticia y Reportaje lleva un único campo country (ISO3), no una lista. El esquema formal está en docs/alert-schema.json.templates/layout.pug carga static/css/layout.css en el <head> (bloqueante) con tokens, tema WB y chrome. El resto de estilos de página viven en static/css/main.css y se incluyen por template. Esto reduce FOUC en la shell de la app.Definiciones: lib/chat/tools.js. Sin orquestación NiFi ni OpenSearch en runtime. Chat depende de LLM configurado; puede alucinar si no usa tools. Cobertura GDELT desigual (HND/GTM más débil). Suscripción guarda email en TSV local; sin envío ni desuscripción automatizada por correo. Strategy 2 (divergencia titular-dato): diseñada, no activa en detección. run_analysis puede tardar minutos y tiene costo LLM. Roadmap: docs/architecture-overview.md, docs/sustainability-plan.md.El demo cubre cinco países de América Latina y el Caribe como prueba de concepto. El conector Data360 es agnóstico de país: los 200+ países que cubre Data360 son el horizonte técnico real, no una promesa futura — el prototipo limita la cobertura por foco, no por restricción arquitectónica.El código es libre bajo GPLv3: cualquier derivado debe seguir siendo un bien público. El core de verificación es y permanecerá accesible para medios de interés público en el Sur Global. El diseño auto-hospedable garantiza soberanía de datos y costo predecible para redacciones en países en desarrollo.Roadmap completo: docs/architecture-overview.md, docs/sustainability-plan.md.

05-sustainability-maintenance-plan.html.title: 05-Sustainability-Maintenance-Plan
05-sustainability-maintenance-plan.html.description:
Status. Final draft for Data360 Global Challenge phase 2 (2026-05-29). "The Challenge is the door to a partner ecosystem, not a terminal prize. The project connects civic tech, media and startups at scale." — Mariano Blejman (Media Party), 2026-05-21 office hour. Data360 News Agent is built to be a public good for public-interest media in the Global South, and a B2B software product for data-publishing institutions. It is the working proof that the World Bank's knowledge bank can be made AI-ready and discoverable for newsrooms — preserving data quality, authoritative sourcing and trustworthiness — without the publisher having to build a single line of bespoke editorial software.The canonical one-liner of the project: An AI-powered news agency that detects newsworthy facts across Data360's 12,000 indicators and delivers them, verified and locally framed, to LAC newsrooms.
The prototype is not a single-deployment showcase. It is the reference instance of a pipeline designed for any institution that publishes a large catalogue of indicators and wants its data to translate into editorial reach — multilateral banks, the IMF, regional commissions like CEPAL, national statistical offices, open-data foundations.The thesis is operational, not aspirational: data portals receive traffic but not coverage. A modern publisher curates thousands of indicators and has no internal newsroom to turn them into stories. Our pipeline performs that conversion — verifiable, bilingual, daily — without the institution having to build editorial capacity in-house, and without compromising newsroom independence on the receiving end.We expect first deployments to fall into one of two configurations:
Both modes preserve the same guarantees: GPLv3 code, CC BY 4.0 documentation, free access for newsrooms and non-profit organisations in developing countries, and AI that augments — not replaces — the editorial workflow. Every story is reviewable, editable and overridable by a human editor before publication. Open forever — pipeline, frontend, documentation, verification trace, and the demo on Data360. Commercial — implementation on a new catalogue, customisation, hosting, training, dedicated editorial support. This is the model that sustains civic-tech infrastructure across the open-source ecosystem, and it is the model that Abrimos.info already operates for other tools in its portfolio (see About Abrimos.info at the end of this document).The Challenge demo is a realistic estimate of unit economics for a World Bank-scale deployment. The numbers below come from the actual reference run run2.log (2026-05-29) and from the live MODEL_PRICING constants in lib/ai-client.js.Sampled Gemini news calls average ~380 input / ~345 output tokens per indicator. Noticia analysis carries the full omnibus context (~18k in / ~2.5k out); Reportaje synthesises multiple Noticias (~35k in / ~5k out).
Prices as of May 2026: Gemini Flash-Lite at $0.10 / $0.40 per 1M input/output tokens; Anthropic Claude Opus 4.7 at $15 / $75 per 1M (pipeline default D-017).A full year of paid-API operation on the current 35-indicator / 5-country scope sits in the ~$2–6k range depending on update density. Scaled to the full Data360 catalogue (12,000 indicators across 200+ countries), dynamic discovery keeps the daily delta below 100 changed indicators in steady state — the economics remain in the low five figures per year, well within institutional budget for a flagship editorial channel.The stack is designed to fall back to $0 marginal cost for clients with budget constraints or strict data-sovereignty requirements:Operational rule: run Gemini + NVIDIA/LAIA for daily replay; reserve Anthropic Opus for final narrative polish or premium tiers where Q1 claim traceability is critical. Self-hosting open-weight models (Llama, Mistral, Qwen) is supported out-of-the-box — relevant for public-sector buyers under European, Brazilian or Singapore AI compliance regimes, and for Global South newsrooms that cannot rely on commercial API budgets. SMTP newsletter — wire subscribers.tsv to a mail provider with the unsubscribe flow already drafted in /privacidad.
NiFi + OpenSearch — replace manual npm replay with scheduled fetch and indexed observations (see architecture-overview.md). Strategy 2 — headline-data divergence as a third detection signal: when headlines and data disagree, that is the story. Self-hosted LLM option — packaged deployment for newsrooms and institutions with strict data-residency requirements. Cross-domain extension (active, externally funded) — see next section.
The same architecture is currently being extended to a second domain — Guatemalan public procurement — under a project funded by the International Press Institute (IPI), in co-design with Ojoconmipisto, a Guatemala City public-interest newsroom. Discovery phase runs April–June 2026; production phase begins July 2026.This matters for sustainability for three reasons: Domain validation in production. The pipeline was designed against Data360 indicators but is not Data360-specific. The IPI extension targets a fundamentally different data shape (Guatecompras procurement records), the same agentic and verification architecture. Generalisation is being proven in production, not on a slide. Independent funding already secured for adjacent work. The roadmap does not depend on the Challenge result. The IPI grant funds the procurement-domain newsletter and the underlying tooling improvements feed back into the Data360 stack — including verification trace generalisation and improvements to the AI-readiness of authoritative public sources. Newsroom co-design pattern. The IPI work is co-designed with the editor team that will operate it, exactly the same operating contract we propose in §1 to data-publishing institutions. The B2B-with-newsroom-co-design pattern has a working precedent inside Abrimos.info. Single Node process behind nginx TLS; optional MCP process on localhost for chat enrichment. No managed cloud dependency beyond LLM API keys. Migratable to fully self-hosted models without code changes.
Features developed during the Challenge — robust REST fallback, freshness probing, omnibus context for narratives — are candidates for contribution to worldbank/data360-mcp. Abrimos maintains a parallel MCP deployment documented in the README; improvements that benefit all MCP consumers will be proposed as pull requests or design notes to the AI for Data team. Strengthening the upstream MCP directly serves Data360's discoverability and AI-readiness goals.Data360 News Agent joins the Abrimos.info consulting catalogue as a defined offering. The commercial model is not improvised — it sits inside a team that has been delivering custom data infrastructure, evidence-based investigation and training for more than a decade, with both public institutions and public-interest newsrooms among its clients (see About Abrimos.info).A recurring fourth source is the Abrimos.info core budget, which covers maintenance of the open staging instance, weekly pipeline replays, and bug-fix releases. None of these revenue lines is required to keep the free tier operational for newsrooms and non-profits in developing countries — the unit economics in §2 make the marginal cost of that free tier negligible against any single paid contract.The product is open source and the verification trace is mechanical: a reader, an editor, or an auditor can reproduce any published claim from Data360 alone, without going through Abrimos or through the institution that funded the deployment. This is the structural answer to the editorial-independence concern that public-interest media funders rightly raise: The funder of an implementation contract has no editorial pre-clearance over what receiving newsrooms publish. The receiving newsroom has full override authority on every generated story before publication.
The verification trace (PCN — Proof-Carrying Numbers, the World Bank's open library that we integrate natively) makes the data lineage independently auditable, so accountability does not depend on trust in either Abrimos or the publisher. Funders fund the pipeline; the newsroom owns the story.Abrimos.info is a distributed civic-tech organisation founded in Mexico, working from and for Latin America for more than a decade. We build digital infrastructure with information that is fundamental for the region's democracies — combining data engineering, narrative work and training. Our consulting catalogue covers data management environments, custom development, evidence-based investigation, training, and secure reporting tools. The Data360 News Agent extends that catalogue into AI-assisted editorial automation for institutional data publishers.Three of our projects are directly relevant to this Challenge:
TeseoETL — open-source ingestion engine. The Data360 connector is built as an extension of TeseoETL, which has been in production handling government data sources since 2019. Battle-tested foundation, not a new experiment.
Sociedad.info — public-procurement and beneficial-ownership explorer for Mexico. Operates today at the scale we project for Data360 News Agent, with the same open-data accountability mission and the same self-hosted, sovereign infrastructure model.
Puebla Open Government Diagnostic — for the Coordinación de Municipio Abierto of the Puebla City Council, we used AI to analyse more than 8,000 access-to-information requests and characterise how the right to information is exercised in one of Mexico's most-petitioned municipalities. Findings included improvements in accessibility, categorisation and response time over recent years, and the observation that the most-asked topics are already published under the city's transparency obligations. This is the closest existing precedent to the B2B model proposed in §1: a public institution contracting Abrimos to apply AI to its own data and produce institutionally usable findings. Full report (PDF).
More about our work and team at abrimos.info.

info_attractionForce.description: How much should linked nodes attract each other? This will make the graph appear more clustered.

info_linkLength.description: How long should the links between nodes be? The shorter the links the more connected nodes will cluster together.

info_repulsionForce.description: How much should nodes repel each other? This will make disconnected parts more spread out.

info_centralForce.description: How much should nodes be attracted to the center? This will make the graph appear more dense and circular.

info_edgePruning.description: Edges with a length above this threshold will not be rendered, however they will still contribute to the simulation. This can help large tangled graphs look more organised. Hovering over a node will still display these links.

info_minNodeRadius.description: How small should the smallest nodes be? The smaller a node is the less it will attract other nodes.

info_maxNodeRadius.description: How large should the largest nodes be? Nodes are sized by how many links they have. The larger a node is the more it will attract other nodes. This can be used to create a good grouping around the most important nodes.
